result:
Done. The spec required three new operations in `CsvRow.cs`, and the build passes cleanly with no warnings.

What I added to `CsvRow.cs`:

- **`EscapeField(string)`** — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow(List<string>)`** — escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- **`SplitRow(string)`** — a single-pass parser: if a field starts with `"` it consumes until an un-doubled closing quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal `"`, and commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise it consumes up to the next comma or end of line. The loop always emits one field per iteration and continues past each separator comma, so the empty line parses to `[""]` and a trailing comma produces a trailing empty field.

This satisfies the round-trip guarantee: `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` reproduces any non-empty field list exactly, with the documented empty-list exception (`JoinRow([])` → `""` → `[""]`). `NeedsQuoting` is untouched, everything is pure computation, and no test or project files were changed. One build cycle used out of ten.
